島群之間 BETWEEN ISLANDS // SIGNALS.2026
[01] BIENNALE CONCEPT [02] SIGNAL LABORATORY [03] EXHIBITION PROGRAM [04] GATE PASS
GRID_SRC: COBALT_WAVE_01 INDEX_CODE: 0x98FF23_TAIWAN_EAST SCALE: 1:1.240.000
2026 Taiwan East Coast Art Exhibition

TAIWAN
EAST COAST
LAND ARTS

An intersection of raw topographical ink paintings and digital sound-wave signals. We capture the physical geometry of Taiwan's eastern cliffs and convert them into interactive visual matrices.

PLATE_01: BETWEEN_ISLANDS BIENNALE EXHIBIT
CONTEMPORARY EAST LAND ART PROJECT 2026 // MONOCHROME PRINT
[SIGNAL_CONTROLLER_v2.6]
REV: 0524

SIGNAL ANALYSIS

Use the dials below to modify parameters. Watch the ocean frequency real-time render map adapt to the input data.

WAVE FREQUENCY: 4.0 Hz RANGE: [1.0 - 10.0]
WAVE AMPLITUDE: 30.0 V RANGE: [5.0 - 80.0]
RENDER MODE: GRID OVERLAYS
MAP_COORDS: [x: 432, y: 108] GRID: ENABLED
STATION: TAITUNG_LAB_01 ● MONITORING SIGNAL STREAMS
// RESEARCH & ARCHIVE

THE COBALT
SPECTRUM OF
EASTERN TAIWAN

Historically, cobalt blue has been used in physical stoneware and architectural drawings to depict structural logic and natural beauty. Our 2026 Biennale takes this historical material and translates it into acoustic wave mechanics.

#0033FF The official color language of the Pacific shorelines under precise moonlit spectrums.
[DIAGRAM_04: COGNITIVE TIDAL NODE MAPPING] SCALE: ACTIVE REALTIME
ACTIVE SCANNER: TAITUNG HARBOR
COORD: LAT 22.75 / LON 121.15 STATUS: STABLE
// EXHIBITIONS & PERFORMANCE STAGES

BIENNALE MODULES

All stages operate in dialogue with natural Pacific tides. Event locations will trigger acoustic generators built in alignment with sea elevations.
[MODULE_01] STAGE: SEA_COAST

MOONLIT SEA CONCERT

Live music echoing across the tide pools of Taitung. Using low-frequency amplifiers that align with incoming lunar swell waves, the performance harmonizes directly with natural marine resonance.

DATE: JUNE 29 – JULY 1 VIEW LINEUP →
[MODULE_02] STAGE: ART_SPACE_III

TIDAL RESONATOR LAB

An indoor contemporary installation translating underwater seismograph frequencies into cobalt blue generative raster matrices. Created in direct collaboration with C-LAB’s technical collective.

DATE: AUGUST 10 – 25 VIEW LAB →
[MODULE_03] STAGE: OPEN_STUDIO

EPHEMERAL LINOCUT STATIONS

Interactive, tactile printmaking workshops on the shore cliffs. Master woodcut creators guide visitors in producing high-contrast block prints using genuine sea salt ink on recycled fibers.

DATE: DAILY ENGAGEMENT JOIN STUDIO →
// COGNITIVE VOICES

EXHIBITING CREATORS

An alliance of sound architects, traditional printmaking masters, and architectural engineers operating inside the eastern coastal grid coordinates.

"We build blueprints, we paint tides, we capture frequency."
[01]

Kuan-Sheng Wu (吳冠勝)

Digital Acoustic Synthesist // Sound Sculptor

RESIDENCY: DURAN STATION [SPECTRAL_MAP_A]
[02]

Min-An Tseng (曾敏安)

Contemporary Blockprint Master // Folk Historian

RESIDENCY: SHANXIANTAI CRAG [BLOCKPRINT_X]
[03]

The C-LAB Signals Collective

Generative Systems & Coding Engineers

RESIDENCY: ART SPACE III [GEO_MATRIX_Z]
[MODULE: REGISTRATION]

GATEWAY GENERATOR

TERM_SESSION: ONLINE

Register for the contemporary arts pass. Custom cryptographic print passes will be compiled immediately with your unique coastal frequency alignment codes.

▶ COMPILER STATUS

System updated with fresh coordinates.

島群之間

A design experiment capturing raw geographic structures and converting them into acoustic, interactive signal installations.

// COORDINATE ARCHIVE

Main Station: Taitung Coastlands, Taiwan

Secondary Hub: Art Space III, Contemporary Culture Lab (C-LAB), Taipei

Broadcasting: 98.4 MHz, Live Streams Active

// LEGAL & TECHNICAL

© 2026 Taiwan East Coast Land Arts Festival. All signal arrays, linocut scans, and frequency generators are open-source. Created in digital visual alignment.
